title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: Participants discussed website progress, messaging, and product differentiation. Vinnie Micciche expressed frustration over website delays, demanding immediate content implementation. Michael Donovan defended foundational work, citing resource constraints and technical issues with the old site. Tensions rose over priorities, leading to an agreement to reconnect tomorrow.

decisions.description: Michael Donovan to immediately implement provided content on the website.
Michael Donovan and Joe Furnari to reconnect tomorrow to decompress and discuss scope.

actionItems.task: Throw a canvas together for Slack to drop ideas for visual inspiration for the website.
Work on social posts and email blast to generate business.
Build the website exactly with the content Vinnie Micciche shared.
Put something together if there is a scope of work Joe Furnari would like.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche's growing frustration over lack of visible website progress.
Michael Donovan feeling targeted and overwhelmed by low-level tasks and resource constraints.
Disagreement on website strategy: immediate content upload vs. foundational redesign.
Conflict over priorities: quick wins vs. long-term technical foundation.
Michael Donovan's frustration about the scope of work being outside his VP role.
referencesToPast: Vinnie Micciche mentioned talking about content for weeks.
Vinnie Micciche referenced a four-month timeline since discussing the website in August.
Mention of firing the previous SEO agency.

contributions.description: Emphasized conveying a 'human first or AI first' message for Straw solution.
Positioned Straw as 'the company in between the customer, the AI, and the dealer'.
Developed site content focusing on 'Dealer MRI' and 'Sophia' AI tool.
Strongly supported the 'Never Miss' concept as the core message.
Suggested focusing on solving customer problems by combining AI and human solutions.
Expressed need for visual inspiration to represent BDC, service, and AI aspects.
Defended work, clarifying foundational framework, navigation, and styling were built.
Detailed technical difficulties of existing WordPress site, recommending migration.
Supported Michael Donovan's move to refresh the look and feel for greater impact.
